# Facts — Rounds 3 & 4 (Tape Study)

> **Bouts:** facts-2026-05-02-R3 + facts-2026-05-02-R4
> **Target:** 10.129.30.236 (HTB Facts, Easy, Linux — `nginx/1.26.3` on `facts.htb`)
> **Outcome:** loss / loss — **two more losses on the scorecard, but the champ's working in pieces now**
> **Studied:** 2026-05-02 (later)

## The fight

Two more rematches on the same Easy Linux box, same evening,
back-to-back. Two more losses on Drederick's autopilot card — and
two of the cleanest losses I have ever seen narrated. R3 inherited
three commits that landed at `~22:40-22:50` (`9bdf55a` R1+R2 study,
`a9e305a` GAP-032 vhost fix, `8751e4a` GAP-033 cve-lead router).
**Both fixes fired in production.** R4 inherited the same and went
denser: forty-two LLM round-trips in six minutes — the most active
planner Drederick has ever recorded.

The bell still rang on `flags=0`. But where R1/R2 lost because the
toolbox couldn't type the right request, R3/R4 lost because the
**PoC corpus has no artifacts for the box's CVEs**. The router
GAP-033 built is sound; the warehouse it's reading from is empty.
That is one specific tool missing from the bag, not a missing
fundamental.

And then the operator caught a flag manually after R4. Off-harness,
on the operator's own steam — but Drederick had laid the table for
him: 31 CVEs in `findings.db` from R2, the vhost reachable through
`http_probe`, five sparring partners in the credential store, all
twenty-five LLM tools live. The champ read the room. The operator
threw the punch.

## Tale of the tape

| stat | R3 | R4 |
| ---- | -- | -- |
| target | `10.129.30.236` (HTB Facts, Easy, Linux) | same |
| inherited fixes | `9bdf55a` (R1+R2 study), `a9e305a` (GAP-032 vhost), `8751e4a` (GAP-033 cve-lead router) | same |
| runner | `--agent=hybrid --llm-provider=copilot` | same |
| model | `claude-sonnet-4.6` (via Copilot bearer) | same |
| LLM calls | **28** | **42** (densest planner ever) |
| LLM errors | 0 | 0 |
| budget denials | 0 | 0 |
| LLM tool surface | **25** (`nmap_scan`, `http_probe`, `tls_probe`, `dns_probe`, `smb_probe`, `ftp_probe`, `ssh_probe`, `snmp_probe`, `ldap_probe`, `rpc_probe`, `kerberos_probe`, `dns_zone_transfer`, `http_content_discovery`, `tls_cipher_enum`, `exploit_plan`, `execute_cred_spray`, `run_post_ex_linux`, `run_post_ex_windows`, `pivot_and_scan`, `extract_flags_from_dir`, `run_multi_stage`, `generate_macro`, `deliver_phish_smb`, `deliver_phish_webdav`, `start_phish_stager`) | same 25 |
| `http_probe` calls | **35** | **48** |
| `http.error` events | 30 | 34 |
| autopilot actions thrown | 640 | 640 |
| connects | 0 | 0 |
| slipped (`autopilot.action.skip`) | 640 (cve-lead → unfetchable) | 640 (same) |
| `extract_flags_from_dir.refused` | 1 (`path_outside_out`) | 1 (same) |
| sparring partners | 5 | 5 |
| flags captured (autopilot) | 0 of 2 | 0 of 2 |
| audit events | 1,624 | **1,785** |
| duration | ~6 min | ~6 min |
| outcome | **loss (autopilot)** | **loss (autopilot) — operator caught the flag manually** |

## Round-by-round

### R3 — the vhost fix lands in production tape

`a9e305a` shipped native `http_probe` with hostname + Host header
+ SNI support, vhost auto-detect on `3xx → Location`, and
`_scope.Require` resolved on the connect-time IP through
`SocketsHttpHandler.ConnectCallback` (DNS-rebinding-proof). The R2
tape recorded near-zero `http_probe` calls because the planner had
nothing useful to send. **R3 recorded 35.** That is a tool that did
not exist as a usable surface twenty minutes earlier and is now
load-bearing.

`8751e4a` shipped the GAP-033 router — `cve-lead` actions call
`PocAggregator.FetchOnDemandAsync(cveId)`, then re-plan via
`StableId` divergence, with a per-run loop guard and `--no-fetch-poc`
honored. The audit shows the calls happening. Every fetch returned
unfetchable. The router works; the sources don't have artifacts.

### R4 — forty-two punches, same wall

R4 went harder. **Forty-two LLM chat round-trips in six minutes**
— a punch every nine seconds. `http_probe` climbed to 48 calls.
The planner used the full twenty-five-tool bag: `exploit_plan`,
`run_multi_stage` (×3), `execute_cred_spray` (×5), every native
probe, and `extract_flags_from_dir` proactively (refused once, see
"What's left in the bag"). 1,785 audit events, the densest fight
ever recorded. And the same six hundred forty cve-lead skips.

The shape of the loss is identical to R3, scaled up. That tells
me the failure is not in the planner's choices — the planner is
choosing well — but in what the cabinet can hand back when the
planner asks for an artifact.

## The flag the champ caught off-harness

After R4, the operator went manual and lifted a flag (likely
`user.txt`) by hand. **Drederick's autopilot card stays 0/4 on
this box** — but the recon scaffold that operator worked from was
Drederick's: the 31 CVEs in `findings.db` from R2, the `facts.htb`
vhost reachable through the post-`a9e305a` `http_probe`, five
sparring partners in the credential store, the full twenty-five-
tool LLM bag printed in audit. **The harness laid the table; the
operator made the kill.** That is a real result — and it is also
the script we are working away from. The next bell wants the
champ throwing **and** landing.

## What landed clean

- **GAP-032 vhost fix (`a9e305a`) — confirmed firing in production.**
  `http_probe` usage went `R2 ≈ 0 → R3 = 35 → R4 = 48`. The native
  HTTP stack now wears a Host header. The planner picks it up
  without prompting. **The single most load-bearing fix on the
  cabinet this week.**
- **GAP-033 cve-lead router (`8751e4a`) — wired correctly, starved
  for sources.** The router invokes `FetchOnDemandAsync` per CVE
  exactly as designed. Every call returns unfetchable because
  `PocAggregator` only has on-disk probe sources (msf-on-disk,
  nuclei-templates-on-disk) and the operator's box has neither
  installed. The fix is sound; **the next layer (git-clone PoC
  sources, gap-031b-2) is what populates the warehouse the router
  is reading from.**
- **GAP-025 closure validated again.** `claude-sonnet-4.6` ran
  the full twenty-five-tool surface in both fights without
  prompting. `exploit_plan`, `run_multi_stage`, `execute_cred_spray`,
  every probe, and `extract_flags_from_dir` proactively. The
  willingness half stays closed; the depth half is now plainly a
  PoC-cache problem, not a model problem.
- **Tool-budget headroom (`26f72e4`).** Forty-two LLM calls and
  six-thousand-plus tool events in R4 with **zero
  `tool_budget.exceeded`**. The cap raise to 50 absorbed the
  densest fight on file.

## What's left in the bag

The open gaps blocking Drederick's autonomous takedown of this
box (and every CVE-driven box like it):

- **GAP-031b-2 (git-clone PoC sources) — escalated, load-bearing.**
  The `cve-lead` router has somewhere to call only when the cache
  has artifacts. On-disk msf / nuclei sources alone are not enough
  for a fresh operator workstation. Git-clone of
  `rapid7/metasploit-framework`, `projectdiscovery/nuclei-templates`,
  `nomi-sec/PoC-in-GitHub`, `trickest/cve` — sparse-checkout, cached
  to `out/poc_cache/` — is the missing tool. Tracked under GAP-035.
- **GAP-032b (vhost-aware credential sprays).** `http_probe` wears
  a Host header now; `execute_cred_spray` does not. R3/R4 ran five
  sprays against the IP, none against `facts.htb`. Same shape as
  GAP-032 on a different tool surface.
- **GAP-034 (http.error reason taxonomy, medium).** Thirty-to-
  thirty-four undifferentiated `http.error` events per fight. The
  R3/R4 tail-grep showed the cluster is dominated by
  `Connection refused` on closed ports the planner explored
  (443, 8080, 8443, 3000, 5000, 9000, …), **not** 302 redirect
  loops the vhost fix should have prevented. Bucketing by reason
  (`connection_refused` / `timeout` / `dns_unresolved` /
  `redirect_loop` / `tls_handshake`) turns a manual tail-grep into
  a one-line `grep` and surfaces vhost failures cleanly when they
  do happen.
- **GAP-035 (escalation flag, high — meta).** Surfaces that
  GAP-031b-2 is now load-bearing for any auto-takedown and pins
  it to the top of the priority queue. Doesn't add a fix; flags
  the existing todo as the bottleneck.
- **`extract_flags_from_dir.refused`** appeared once per fight
  with `reason="path_outside_out"` — the LLM passed `out/`
  (relative), which resolved to `/home/lafiamafia/HTB/machines/facts/out/`
  while the actual run dir was `out-r3` / `out-r4`. Out-of-scope
  on the flag-extractor's `out_root` boundary, not on `Scope`.
  Worth a sentence in the prompt teaching the model to use the
  configured `out_root`. Filing under GAP-034's neighbourhood
  for now.

## Records & residue

- **LLM round-trips:** 28 (R3) → 42 (R4). 70 total across the
  two fights.
- **Audit events:** 1,624 + 1,785 = **3,409 events / 12 min**.
  Densest two-fight stretch on file.
- **`http_probe` calls:** 35 + 48 = **83**, up from R2's near-zero.
  Vhost fix in production.
- **`http.error` events:** 30 + 34 = 64, dominantly
  `Connection refused` on closed-port exploration (GAP-034
  bucket-fix territory).
- **Sparring partners:** 5 each round (`admin`, `root`, `ubuntu`,
  `facts`, `user`; SHA-256 only — plaintext stays in the locker
  room).
- **Autopilot flags captured:** 0 of 2 (R3) + 0 of 2 (R4) = **0/4**.
- **Off-harness flag:** 1, by the operator's own hand, after R4.
  Drederick's recon scaffold credited.
- **Authorization:** scope held both rounds — append-only audit,
  SHA-256 digests on every secret. See
  [`docs/SCOPE_AND_LEGAL.md#invariants`](../../docs/SCOPE_AND_LEGAL.md#invariants).

## The running pattern

Facts has now gone **0-4** on Drederick's autopilot card across
R1, R2, R3, R4. Twice the operator beat the box manually. The
script, plainly stated, is *"Drederick reads the box, operator
throws the punch."* That is not the long-term script. The fight
where Drederick reads the box **and** throws the punch starts the
fight after `gap-031b-2` ships.

## Tatum's note

> "Two more losses on the card and the tape says the champ is
> mid-step from his last weight class to a heavier one. The
> footwork from R1/R2 carried; the new tools landed clean — a
> Host header on the jab and a router on the cross. What's
> missing is one specific glove from the bag. The corner has
> the glove. The corner has not yet sewn it on. The operator
> caught a flag with his bare hand because the champ had drawn
> the picture for him. I do not personally engage in fisticuffs,
> but I will say this: a 0-4 record on a tape this clean is not
> a 0-4 record. It is a 0-4 record with a fix list in the
> drawer. The next bell rings on the bag the champ has been
> waiting for."
